name: Begin / End
desc: Top-level window scope. Begin() starts a window and returns visibility/collapse state for content submission optimization. End() must always be called for every Begin(), even when Begin returns false.
note: Core Dear ImGui rule from docs/FAQ + imgui.h comments: Begin/End are the exception to other BeginX/EndX APIs. Most other pairs require EndX only when BeginX returns true, but Begin/End always pair.
alsoknow: Use SetNextWindowPos/Size/Collapsed/Focus before Begin for deterministic startup layout
Window flags worth knowing: NoResize, NoMove, NoScrollbar, AlwaysAutoResize, MenuBar

name: Text
desc: Read-only text output. Accepts printf-style formatting and supports wrapped, colored, disabled, and label/value variants.
note: For std::string use c_str() or %s formatter: ImGui::Text("%s", s.c_str()). TextUnformatted is more efficient for very long immutable blobs because it avoids formatting overhead.
alsoknow: LabelText draws value-like pairs aligned with input widgets
SeparatorText gives semantic section headers

name: Button
desc: Clickable widget returning true on activation frame. Primary action trigger in immediate mode UIs.
note: ID system is hash-based over label + ID stack. Duplicate labels in same scope must be disambiguated via PushID() or ##suffix; otherwise interaction conflicts occur.
alsoknow: SmallButton for inline controls
InvisibleButton for custom-drawn interactions

name: Checkbox / RadioButton
desc: Boolean and single-choice toggles. Checkbox binds to bool pointer. RadioButton supports explicit group semantics.
note: Checkbox returns true only on state change frame. You can use that return to trigger side effects (rebuild graph, write history marker).
alsoknow: Use BeginDisabled/EndDisabled to make controls non-interactive while preserving layout
Use SetItemTooltip for discoverability on compact icon-only toggles

name: Slider / Drag
desc: Numeric editing with pointer-bound values. Slider gives bounded track control; Drag allows velocity-based value changes and often feels better for pro tools.
note: Ctrl+click opens direct text entry for slider/drag widgets. Useful flags: AlwaysClamp, Logarithmic, NoRoundToFormat, WrapAround (Drag only).
alsoknow: Use DragFloatRange2/DragIntRange2 for bounded ranges
For vector params use SliderFloat2/3/4 and DragScalarN

name: InputText
desc: Editable UTF-8 text entry. Works with mutable char buffers by default; wrappers exist for std::string in misc/cpp/imgui_stdlib.h.
note: For dynamic strings, use callback resize mode or imgui_stdlib wrapper. Useful callback flags: CallbackCompletion, CallbackHistory, CallbackCharFilter, CallbackResize.
alsoknow: InputTextMultiline for editors/notes
InputFloat/InputInt family for typed numeric input

name: BeginCombo / ListBox
desc: Selection widgets. BeginCombo gives full control over item generation and filtering. ListBox is useful for persistent visible lists in constrained spaces.
note: BeginCombo/EndCombo pattern is more future-proof than old Combo helper overloads because you can inject filtering, grouping, icons, and custom row visuals.
alsoknow: Use HeightSmall/HeightLarge combo flags to control popup size
ListBox is essentially framed child region + selectable rows

name: TreeNode / CollapsingHeader
desc: Hierarchical disclosure controls. TreeNode manages nested scopes and requires TreePop when open; CollapsingHeader is flat and does not push ID/indent scope.
note: TreeNode flags that matter: DefaultOpen, OpenOnArrow, OpenOnDoubleClick, SpanAvailWidth, Framed. For dense inspector UIs, CollapsingHeader usually yields cleaner ergonomics.
alsoknow: SetNextItemOpen controls next tree/header open state
TreeNode IDs follow same label/ID stack rules

name: SameLine / Spacing / Dummy / Separator
desc: Core layout micro-primitives for horizontal grouping, vertical rhythm, and explicit pixel-reserved space.
note: SameLine(x, spacing): x is absolute local x when non-zero; spacing controls gap when x is zero. Dummy is the most deterministic spacing primitive for pixel-accurate UI.
alsoknow: Indent/Unindent for nested groups
AlignTextToFramePadding to align labels with framed widgets

name: BeginChild
desc: Nested scrolling/clipping region with independent content flow. Great for sidebars, logs, pattern lists, and inspectors inside a main window.
note: Newer API uses ImGuiChildFlags for behavior/styling (Borders, AutoResizeX/Y, ResizeX/Y, FrameStyle, NavFlattened). EndChild is always required once BeginChild is called.
alsoknow: Pass height 0 to fill remaining vertical space
Combine with NoScrollbar window flag if you want strict fixed viewport content

name: BeginTable
desc: Modern table API for robust alignment, resizing, sorting, reordering, clipping, and scrollable grids.
note: Call EndTable only when BeginTable returns true. For large datasets combine table with ImGuiListClipper to avoid per-row submission cost when offscreen.
alsoknow: TableSetupScrollFreeze freezes header rows/columns during scroll
Use TableSetBgColor for row/column/cell overlays
Sort specs via TableGetSortSpecs for user-driven multi-column sorting

name: TableGetSortSpecs
desc: Pulls user sorting preferences from sortable tables. You sort your own data model when SpecsDirty is true and then clear the dirty flag.
note: Sorting is application-owned by design. ImGui emits intent; your code applies ordering on data and redraws.
alsoknow: Enable SortMulti for shift-multi-sort
Enable SortTristate for unsorted state

name: Popup / Modal
desc: Transient overlays for contextual actions and confirmations. BeginPopupContextItem is ideal for right-click menus; BeginPopupModal is for blocking confirmations.
note: Popup IDs are scoped by ID stack, so OpenPopup and BeginPopup should typically execute in consistent scopes. For context menus, use unique IDs per row/item.
alsoknow: MenuItem auto-closes popup unless disabled by item/popup flags
IsPopupOpen supports AnyPopup and AnyPopupLevel checks

name: BeginMenuBar / BeginMenu / MenuItem
desc: Hierarchical menu system for app commands. Can be attached to window menu bars or global main menu bar.
note: MenuItem shortcut text is display-only by default; actual key handling is your responsibility (Shortcut API is recommended).
alsoknow: Window-level menu bar requires ImGuiWindowFlags_MenuBar + BeginMenuBar
Use BeginDisabled inside menus for unavailable commands

name: BeginTabBar / BeginTabItem
desc: Tab interfaces for organizing panels and editors without separate windows.
note: Tab item EndTabItem only when BeginTabItem returns true. Useful tab bar flags: Reorderable, AutoSelectNewTabs, NoCloseWithMiddleMouseButton.
alsoknow: TabItemButton creates tab-like buttons that are not selectable tabs
SetTabItemClosed reduces flicker when external close events occur

name: Selectable / Multi-Select
desc: Row/item selection primitive. New multi-select APIs support Ctrl/Shift idioms with app-owned selection storage.
note: For large virtualized lists, pair multi-select with clipper and stable user data IDs. Selection ownership remains in your model, not in ImGui internals.
alsoknow: IsItemToggledSelection helps detect per-row changes before EndMultiSelect
Selectable flags: SpanAllColumns, AllowDoubleClick, Highlight

name: PushID / PopID / ##
desc: Identifier system fundamentals. IDs define interaction state ownership. In loops, collisions must be avoided with PushID or hidden label suffixes.
note: Empty visible labels are legal but still need IDs (e.g., "##gain"). FAQ explicitly warns that Button("") collides with parent scope ID and should be avoided.
alsoknow: ShowIDStackToolWindow is invaluable when debugging ID collisions
GetID can be used for custom storage keys

name: PushStyleColor
desc: Scoped color overrides for widget rendering. Stack-based and deterministic.
note: Always pop exactly what you push. A mismatch can poison style state for the rest of the frame/session and is a common source of hard-to-trace UI bugs.
alsoknow: GetColorU32 returns style-aware packed color useful for drawlist
Use SetColorEditOptions once for color-widget behavior defaults

name: PushStyleVar
desc: Scoped overrides for spacing/rounding/padding and other style geometry parameters.
note: StyleVar enum value determines expected payload type (float or ImVec2). Passing wrong type fails at compile time.
alsoknow: For global theme setup, mutate ImGui::GetStyle once at startup
ButtonTextAlign/SelectableTextAlign can improve dense control readability

name: GetWindowDrawList
desc: Low-level immediate drawing (lines, rects, circles, text, images) in current window draw channel.
note: Draw list uses absolute screen coordinates. After custom draw, submit a Dummy of same size to advance layout cursor and prevent overlap with subsequent widgets.
alsoknow: Use PushClipRect/PopClipRect for custom clipped rendering
Foreground/background draw lists are available for global overlays

name: BeginDragDropSource / BeginDragDropTarget
desc: Payload-based drag and drop. Source publishes typed data; target accepts matching payload type and consumes it on delivery.
note: Payload type is a short user-defined string (max 32 chars). Keep it stable and namespaced to avoid accidental cross-feature collisions.
alsoknow: AcceptBeforeDelivery lets you preview target handling while hovering
NoDrawDefaultRect disables built-in highlight if you draw your own

name: ImGuiListClipper
desc: Large-list coarse clipping helper. Submits only visible rows while preserving scroll metrics and keyboard navigation behavior.
note: Use when row count is large (thousands+). It reduces CPU spent on widget submission for offscreen items, which is often the dominant cost in heavy inspector/list UIs.
alsoknow: Works well with tables and multi-select APIs
IncludeItemsByIndex can force critical rows to stay submitted

name: ImGuiIO Capture Flags
desc: Integration contract between app event routing and ImGui. io.WantCaptureMouse / WantCaptureKeyboard tell your host app when to consume or pass through input.
note: Official guidance: check capture flags before NewFrame for correct behavior. Do not infer capture using IsWindowHovered/IsItemHovered for global input routing decisions.
alsoknow: WantTextInput is useful for spawning virtual keyboard on mobile/console
ConfigInputTrickleEventQueue helps low-FPS interaction quality

name: Tooltip APIs
desc: Contextual hover help. Can be lightweight text or full custom widget trees in a tooltip window.
note: Tooltip hover behavior is tunable via style hover delay/stationary flags. Use BeginItemTooltip for richer content and consistent default hover semantics.
alsoknow: SetItemTooltip is concise for common one-liners
Tooltips are excluded from disabled state by default

name: Shortcut / Key APIs
desc: Keyboard shortcut routing and conflict resolution. Preferred modern API over direct IsKeyPressed checks for command accelerators.
note: Shortcut API participates in route ownership and focused-scope resolution, reducing accidental conflicts in large apps with nested tools and plugins.
alsoknow: SetNextItemShortcut annotates upcoming item with displayed shortcut
Use RouteGlobal/RouteFocused flags for explicit shortcut ownership policy

name: Debug / Metrics Tools
desc: Built-in diagnostics windows for UI tree, draw calls, ID stack, and runtime logs. Essential when debugging interaction issues.
note: ID conflicts, clipping issues, and unexpected focus/capture behavior are significantly easier to resolve with these tools enabled during development builds.
alsoknow: DebugStartItemPicker lets you click an item and inspect internals
ConfigDebugHighlightIdConflicts can surface duplicate-ID bugs early

name: Font Atlas Basics
desc: Load and manage fonts through ImFontAtlas; switch fonts with PushFont/PopFont. Dear ImGui now supports dynamic sizing with baked font data per size.
note: If your backend supports RendererHasTextures (1.92+ path), atlas texture updates are managed through ImTextureData request flow. For legacy backends, SetTexID path still exists.
alsoknow: Use imgui_stdlib and FONTS.md guidance for Unicode-heavy apps
Glyph ranges and memory ownership rules are frequent sources of mistakes

name: BeginDisabled / EndDisabled
desc: Temporarily disables interaction and dims visuals for enclosed controls while preserving layout and state display.
note: Great for command gating and mode-dependent controls. Disabled items can still be hovered with AllowWhenDisabled flags when needed for explanatory tooltips.
alsoknow: Can be nested; any active disable in stack keeps item disabled
Prefer over manually guarding every widget for cleaner code

name: SeparatorText / Group
desc: Section organization and grouping primitives for readability in dense panels.
note: BeginGroup/EndGroup lets a collection behave like one logical item for hover/layout interactions, useful for custom context menus and drag zones.
alsoknow: GetItemRectMin/Max after EndGroup gives group bounding box
Pair with DrawList to render custom backgrounds around grouped controls
